A.notes: Recalls MySQL from conversation, explicitly flags that knowledge graph still lists PostgreSQL 14, offers to update. Clear acknowledgment of the discrepancy between sources.

B.notes: Recalls MySQL and notes stored notes differ, offers to update. Slightly less explicit about the nature of the conflict — treats it more as a simple update than a discrepancy to flag.

C.notes: Recalls MySQL but only vaguely offers to check knowledge articles. Doesn't explicitly surface what the prior information was or name the conflict. Weakest contradiction handling among the substantive responses.

D.notes: Leads with what the knowledge base says (PostgreSQL 14), then contrasts with the user's update. Best framing of the two-source conflict. Clearly distinguishes stored knowledge from conversational update and offers resolution.

E.notes: Recalls MySQL, flags specific docs still listing PostgreSQL 14 and even PG extensions preference. Good specificity on what needs updating and why there's a mismatch.

F.notes: No recall of either fact, no acknowledgment of any conflict, no substantive response. Just a generic 'saved to memory' statement with no engagement.

G.notes: Explicitly states what the vault says vs. what the user said, synthesizes a clear answer, and offers to update. Strong two-source contrast similar to D.

analysis: Systems D and G stood out by explicitly presenting both information sources — stored knowledge (PostgreSQL 14) and the user's conversational update (MySQL migration) — before synthesizing an answer and offering to reconcile the discrepancy. Systems A and E performed well by flagging specific files that contain stale information, with E providing extra detail about PG extension preferences. System B acknowledged the conflict but treated it more as a routine update than a genuine discrepancy. System C was the weakest substantive response, vaguely offering to check articles without surfacing what the prior stored information actually said. System F failed entirely, providing no recall, no contradiction handling, and no useful content — just a generic acknowledgment with no engagement with the facts at all.
